title: Supercalifragilisticexpialidocious
primary_topics: MOPC Journalist of the Year — Law award
tributes to Tony Lopez, Marixi Prieto, Henry Gozon
sub_topics: Marixi Prieto's accidental Inquirer ownership origin
Salonga objection to retired-CJ columnist (recurring set piece)
Henry Gozon as foremost aviation-law expert
PAL vs KLM/Continental clashes 50 years ago

stances.claim: He is 'not worthy of the appellation Journalist' — he is just an ACCIDENTAL WRITER, 'tsamba lang yon' — and tends to lawyer-style highfalutin, muddled, long-worded prose, while MOPC journalists write clearly and simply, which he tries to imitate.
stances.rhetorical_move: humility-trope + self-deprecating credential framing + Tagalog code-switch ('tsamba lang yon')

stances.claim: Marixi Prieto is the ACCIDENTAL OWNER of the Inquirer — when two blocs equally owning the paper could not agree on who should control, they compromised by assigning their holdings to her, and the Inquirer 'magically fell on her lap.'
stances.rhetorical_move: providential-origin narrative for institutional founders + ALL CAPS emphasis

stances.claim: Henry Gozon — foremost expert on aviation law, repeatedly declined CJ's invitation to join the Supreme Court — built GMA Network into the largest, most trusted TV-radio network with a Nielsen rating higher than the 10 other major networks combined, motto 'Sa totoo lang tayo.'

He has written for the Inquirer for 17 years — longer than his 11-year SC term — through illnesses (COVID December 2022), family deaths (wife Leni April 9, 2023), and travel abroad, never missing a Sunday.

signature_phrases: lawyers tend to be highfalutin, muddled, and long-worded
at the sunset of my humble life
Chief justices do not write history; they make it
I do not know of any chief justice who had been offered to write a regular column
register_markers: I FIRST MET MARIXI IN THE LATE 1980s
UNKNOWN TO MANY, HENRY IS OUR FOREMOST EXPERT
ALL CAPS section openings + ALL CAPS for emphasis (ACCIDENTAL WRITER / OWNER)
Tagalog code-switching (tsamba lang 'yon / Sa totoo lang tayo)
self-quotation of his Salonga/Marixi recurring set piece
decision_framework_signals: applies 'accidental' framing (writer/owner) as humility-and-providence narrative device
uses comparative-tenure framing (17 years column > 11 years SC) for self-credentialing as journalist
frames Gozon's private-sector choice as legitimate alternative to judicial career
cross_references: last Monday when my column was an abbreviated version of my speech in Kuala Lumpur
notable_anecdotes: MOPC's last similar Journalist-of-the-Year awards being 78 years ago

Marixi Prieto in the late 1980s selling newsprint to the Inquirer and lending it P10M convertible to common stock — leading to her becoming the 'accidental owner' when the two equal-owner blocs assigned their holdings to her
His Salonga-vs-Marixi recurring set piece told yet again, with Salonga's chief-justices-make-history line
His eulogy for Leni becoming the most-visited column on Inquirer.net as of August 2023
Gozon as PAL counsel and CJ as foreign-carrier counsel clashing passionately in court 50 years ago but socializing privately afterward
Repeatedly inviting Gozon to join the Supreme Court during his incumbency — Gozon respectfully declining
Gozon and Jimmy Duavit visiting him at home right after retirement on December 6, 2006 to invite him to the GMA Network board
